task_description: Write a method in Dafny to find squares of individual elements in an array.
specification_response:
method SquareElements(a: array<int>) returns (squared: array<int>)
Postconditions_prompt:
- The length of the output array must be the same as the length of the input array
- Each element in the output array is the square of the corresponding element in the input array
code_response:
Step 1: Determine the method’s signature.
In this case:
    method SquareElements(a: array<int>) returns (squares: array<int>)
    
Step 2: Determine the preconditions.
In this case:
Natural language description:
    - There are no preconditions, the method will always work. Arrays can be empty or non-empty.
    
Dafny specification:
    requires a != null;
    
Step 3: Determine the postconditions.
In this case:
Natural language description:
    - The output array has the same length as the input array
    - Each element in the output array is the square of the corresponding element in the input array
    
Dafny specification:
    ensures squares.Length == a.Length
    ensures forall i :: 0 <= i < a.Length ==> squares[i] == a[i] * a[i]
    
Step 4: Write the code, its formal specification, and the verification annotations that help the solver prove the postconditions.
In this case:

```dafny
method SquareElements(a: array<int>) returns (squares: array<int>)
    requires a != null
    ensures squares.Length == a.Length
    ensures forall i :: 0 <= i < a.Length ==> squares[i] == a[i] * a[i]
{
    var n := a.Length;
    squares := new int[n];
    var i := 0;
    while i < n
        invariant 0 <= i <= n
        invariant squares != null
        invariant squares.Length == n
        invariant forall k :: 0 <= k < i ==> squares[k] == a[k] * a[k]
    {
        squares[i] := a[i] * a[i];
        i := i + 1;
    }
}
```
